svg.text: ZULU
NGUNI CLICK PHONOLOGY
ZULU CLICK CONSONANTS
Dental click /|/ (c): tongue tip against upper teeth, release forward
Palatal click /!/ (q): tongue body against palate, release upward
Lateral click /ɘ/ (x): tongue sides, release laterally (tsk sound)
Each click has voiced (g-), nasalized (ng-), and aspirated (kh-) forms
ZULU TONES
Zulu is tonal: High vs Low tones (not marked in standard spelling)
Tone is phonemic: umlilo "fire" vs. umlilo (different tones = diff. word)
Tone changes with verb tense: present vs. past tones differ
ZULU VOWELS & CONSONANTS
5 vowels: a e i o u (short only; no long vowels)
No aspiration contrast in stops (unlike English)
Rich fricative set: f v s z sh hl dl h
XHOSA PHONOLOGY · SECONDARY LANGUAGE
XHOSA CLICKS
Same three click types as Zulu (c, q, x) plus more combinations
Xhosa has ~15 distinct click phonemes vs Zulu ~12
Additional: breathy-voiced clicks (ghc, etc.) in some analyses
Click frequency higher in Xhosa than Zulu in everyday speech
XHOSA TONES
Also tonal: High vs Low (same binary system as Zulu)
Tone marks verb tenses and noun classes in parallel to Zulu
XHOSA UNIQUE FEATURES
Has /r/ phoneme (Zulu does not); used in Xhosa loanwords
Implosive /ɓ/ (b with downward airflow) in some dialects
Slightly more retroflex sounds than Zulu
SHARED NGUNI PHONOLOGY
Both: 3 click types (dental, palatal, lateral)
Both: binary H/L tone system • both: 5 vowels
Both: prenasalized stops (mb, nd, ng, nj)
Clicks inherited from Khoisan contact during southern migration
PLATE 37b ALT · ZULU / XHOSA · PHONOLOGY
